fresh_facts.claim: The Pentagon plans to deploy 3,000–4,000 soldiers from the U.S. Army's 82nd Airborne Division to unspecified Middle East locations, building on prior forces of 50,000 troops.

Approximately 2,500 Marines deployed on USS Boxer and accompanying warships under Operation Epic Fury departed San Diego last week, focused on embassy support, evacuations, and securing areas like the Strait of Hormuz.
Israeli strikes since February 28 displaced over 1 million Lebanese (20% of population) and killed 968 people in Lebanon, with 15 killed in Israel via Iranian missiles.
The United States has struck approximately 9,000 Iranian targets including missiles, navy, and infrastructure.
Thirteen U.S. troops have been killed and 290 wounded (255 returned to duty) in the conflict.
Over 1,300 people have been killed in Iran during the conflict.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei died on February 28, 2026.
Mojtaba Khamenei, the Supreme Leader's son, has been selected as his successor.
Iran's leadership appointed Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr as secretary of the Supreme National Security Council, signaling a security-first approach to governance.
Israeli and U.S. strikes targeted Iranian military infrastructure across Tehran, Isfahan, Shiraz, Bandar Abbas, and Mashhad over the past 24 hours.
The IDF struck weapons production facilities in the Tehran area.
Iranian missiles reached areas near Israel's Dimona facility, and Gulf states intercepted additional Iranian projectiles.
A 15-point U.S. proposal was presented to Iran via Pakistan on March 24, 2026.

President Trump announced a 10-day suspension of strikes against Iranian power plants until April 6, 2026, at 8 p.m. Eastern time, following what he characterized as a request from the Iranian government.
President Trump stated that military operations against Iranian targets other than power plants would continue.
Kuwait announced the arrest of six people linked to Hezbollah for allegedly plotting to assassinate state leaders.
The conflict has entered a grinding middle phase in which neither battlefield momentum nor diplomacy has yet become decisive.
Iranian command, naval, and industrial capacity are being systematically degraded during the conflict.
A critical standoff persists over the Strait of Hormuz with Trump's earlier threat to strike Iranian power stations if Tehran does not fully reopen the strait.
Kharg Island, which accounts for 90% of Iran's oil exports, has been referenced as a potential military target.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces acute military pressure from sustained U.S. and Israeli strikes targeting infrastructure, concurrent with a leadership succession following Supreme Leader Khamenei's death on February 28. A 10-day diplomatic pause on power plant strikes offers a narrow window for negotiation, but military operations continue elsewhere.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Israeli and U.S. strikes targeted Iranian military infrastructure across multiple cities (Tehran, Isfahan, Shiraz, Bandar Abbas, Mashhad) on March 27, with weapons production facilities struck and Iranian missiles reaching near Dimona.
